pi_items.source: Web search confirmed Miami is in IALA Region B (Americas region including North, Central, and South America, Japan, Philippines, Korea)
Web search confirmed 'Red Right Returning' is the correct mnemonic for IALA Region B (https://sailing-blog.nauticed.org/iala-a-and-iala-b-navigation-marks-and-atons/)

IALA Region B specification: North (Starboard/Right) side when returning to port is marked by Red buoys. For a Westbound vessel (heading 270 degrees), the North side is Starboard. Web search confirmed IALA Region B assigns red marks to starboard when entering/returning (https://support.nauticed.org/support/solutions/articles/44002538908-iala-international-buoyage-system-red-and-green-buoys)

IALA Region B specification: South (Port/Left) side when returning to port is marked by Green buoys. For a Westbound vessel, the South side is Port. Web search confirmed IALA Region B assigns green marks to port when entering/returning (https://support.nauticed.org/support/solutions/articles/44002538908-iala-international-buoyage-system-red-and-green-buoys)

Web search found actual Government Cut, Miami Harbor Entrance coordinates: 25.7633° N, -80.1300° W (from https://www.usharbors.com/harbor/florida/government-cut-miami-harbor-entrance-fl/map/). Alternative sources list 25.76389, 25.762697, or N 25° 45.853' (= 25.7641° decimal). Task claims 25.766 which differs by ~0.002-0.003 degrees (~200-300 meters).

Web search found actual Government Cut, Miami Harbor Entrance coordinates: -80.1300° W (some sources: -80.13556, or W 080° 08.005' = -80.1334° decimal). Task claims -80.080 which differs by ~0.05 degrees (~3-5 km). This is a significant discrepancy from the actual Government Cut entrance location.
Cannot verify - these are synthetic coordinates used for the simulated Bridge Command scenario, not based on real Government Cut buoy positions
Cannot verify - these are synthetic coordinates for buoys in the simulated scenario. Not based on real Government Cut buoy positions.
Cannot verify - these are synthetic coordinates for buoys in the simulated scenario. Not based on real Government Cut buoy positions.
Configuration path from setup_task.sh and export_result.sh - this is the intended installation location in Bridge Command

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic Bridge Command scenario simulating the entrance to Miami Government Cut. VERIFIED facts: (1) Miami is in IALA Region B, confirmed from nautical standards documentation; (2) 'Red Right Returning' is the correct mnemonic for Region B; (3) In IALA Region B when returning to port, Starboard (North for a Westbound vessel) is marked Red and Port (South) is marked Green. CONTRADICTED: The task claims the entrance is at coordinates 25.766 N, -80.080 W, but the actual Government Cut entrance is at 25.7633 N, -80.1300 W—a discrepancy of approximately 200-300 meters in latitude and 3-5 km in longitude. The other coordinate values (gate longitudes, buoy latitudes) are unverifiable as they represent synthetic simulation data rather than real port data.
